Changelog

taxodist 0.5.0 Latest

New features

  • taxobase: Built-in reference dataset of 50 taxonomic clades with pre-computed distance matrices, lineage paths, search outputs, and coverage vectors. Acts as a complete offline fallback — examples, vignettes, and unit tests run without a network connection.

Bug fixes

  • Fixed taxa whose names contain the substring “planet” (e.g. Periplaneta) being incorrectly discarded by the astronomical homonym filter. The filter now uses word boundaries (\bplanet\b).
  • Fixed a lineage parsing bug affecting genera whose Taxonomicon page does not link back to the genus itself (e.g. Periplaneta). The lineage is now correctly truncated before child species entries and the genus name is appended if absent.
  • Added "Epifamily" to the bare rank token filter.
  • Fixed a lineage parsing bug where the superscript type-marker ᵀ (U+1D40) immediately following a taxon name prevented word-boundary matching, causing the lineage to overshoot the target node and include descendant taxa as ancestors. This bug disproportionately affected higher-rank taxa within deep clades like Dinosauria.
  • Fixed a lineage parsing bug where navigation header text could contain the target taxon name, causing the lineage cutoff to land in junk content rather than the taxonomic tree (Drosophila, ID 28940, was one affected case).

taxodist 0.4.0

New features

  • cache_info(): Inspects the current session cache, reporting the number of cached lineages and IDs, total memory used, and cached taxon names. Returns a named list invisibly for programmatic use.
  • focal_distances(): Computes distances from a single focal taxon to all members of a community vector. Returns a sorted data frame with columns taxon, distance, mrca, and mrca_depth.

Bug fixes

  • Fixed a lineage parsing bug where taxa with Subphylum and Infraphylum rank prefixes (e.g. Craniata, Vertebrata) were incorrectly discarded. The dagger symbol (†) is now stripped before rank-prefix removal.
  • Fixed parsing of auct. author annotations preventing rank-prefixed names from being correctly cleaned.
  • Fixed intermediate clades being silently dropped when author strings contained ampersands, hyphens (e.g. Cavalier-Smith), or trailing commas without a year.
  • Fixed a bug where the dagger extinction marker (†) appearing between a rank prefix and a taxon name (e.g. Family † Dromaeosauridae) caused the taxon to be lost entirely.

taxodist 0.3.0 First Python release

New features

  • taxo_search(): Interactive search function that queries The Taxonomicon and returns a tidy data frame of all available IDs, ranks, and authors for a given taxon name.
  • Direct ID support: get_lineage() and all distance functions now accept Taxonomicon numeric IDs as a fallback for homonyms and historical ranks that cannot be disambiguated by name alone.

Network handling

  • Added a 30-second timeout to all HTTP requests to prevent the session from hanging when The Taxonomicon servers are overloaded.
  • Network failures, offline servers, and bad HTTP statuses now emit a clear, informative warning rather than failing silently.

Bug fixes

  • Extended rank-prefix filter to cover additional bare rank tokens: Subgenus, Section, Division, Subdivision, Supercohort, Infracohort, Subsection, Candidatus, Parvphylum, Branch, and the navigation artefact Go to.
  • Duplicate lineage nodes from data-quality issues in The Taxonomicon are now collapsed to a single occurrence. A warning is emitted when deduplication occurs.
  • get_taxonomicon_id() now collects all biological matches before returning and emits a warning when multiple valid entries are found, surfacing homonym ambiguity (e.g. Nereis matching both a polychaete worm and a butterfly genus).
  • get_taxonomicon_id() now properly follows taxonomic redirects and removes interface noise from warnings.
  • Lineage parsing is now stricter, truncating philosophical or pre-basal nodes (e.g. organisms) appearing before Biota.
  • Added validation to get_lineage_by_id() to silently return NULL for non-numeric strings.
  • Fixed erroneous truncation of the parent Genus from species-level queries. Distances and MRCAs between congeneric species are now computed accurately.
  • Fixed an issue where author names enclosed in double quotes were evading the lineage cleaning pipeline and being retained as part of the clade name.

taxodist 0.2.0

New functions

  • taxo_cluster(): Hierarchical clustering of taxa by taxonomic distance.
  • taxo_ordinate(): Ordination (PCoA) of taxa in taxonomic distance space.
  • save_cache(): Serializes the session lineage cache to an .rds file.
  • load_cache(): Restores a previously saved cache, avoiding redundant network requests.
  • taxo_path(): Returns the full node-by-node path between two taxa as a tidy data frame, ascending from taxon A to their MRCA and descending to taxon B.

Documentation

  • Added a new vignette (Statistical Applications of taxodist) demonstrating integration with ape (tree plotting) and vegan (taxonomic distinctness, Mantel tests, and PERMANOVA).

Minor improvements

  • Added vegan to Suggests in DESCRIPTION.
  • Documented compatibility of distance_matrix() output with vegan functions (taxondive(), mantel(), adonis2()).
  • Documented conversion of taxo_cluster() results into phylo objects using ape::as.phylo().

Bug fixes

  • Fixed incorrect MRCA computation caused by unnamed crown nodes ([crown]) being matched across different lineages as if they were the same ancestor.
  • Fixed parsing of Taxonomicon rank prefixes (Cohort, Subcohort, Magnorder, Grandorder, Parvorder, Legion) that were being retained as bare rank names instead of the actual clade names.

taxodist 0.1.0 Initial release

  • Compute taxonomic distances between any two taxa using The Taxonomicon.
  • Session-level caching for lineage data to minimize network requests.
  • Initial function set: taxo_distance(), mrca(), distance_matrix(), closest_relative(), compare_lineages(), shared_clades(), is_member(), filter_clade(), lineage_depth(), check_coverage(), clear_cache().
